title: acer campbellii subsp. campbellii
intro: Acer campbellii subsp. campbellii, also known as the Himalayan maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species belonging to the family Sapindaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The extracts from this plant have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The herbal preparations of Acer campbellii subsp. campbellii typically involve its leaves and bark, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
